title: Bridging Talk and Thought: Understanding Dialogue Dynamics Across Collaborative Problem-Solving Contexts
abstract: arXiv:2606.27233v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: We present a conceptual framework for analyzing dialogue in collaborative problem-solving contexts, with an emphasis on the emerging dynamics of human-AI and multi-agent collaboration. As intelligent systems become active agents capable of autonomous reasoning and strategic cooperation, understanding the dialogic interaction during collaborative problem solving is increasingly important for optimizing and evaluating such partnerships. Our framework addresses key limitations in current analytical approaches through a hierarchical two-layer coding scheme that integrates cognitive and non-cognitive problem solving with metacognitive regulatory mechanisms. We demonstrate its effectiveness and generalizability across nine datasets spanning multiple domains, and provide insights into how humans and agents coordinate their knowledge, skills, and efforts to solve complex problems, showing in particular that metacognitive regulation can be an e

Title: Bridging Talk and Thought: Understanding Dialogue Dynamics Across Collaborative Problem-Solving Contexts
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27233v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: We present a conceptual framework for analyzing dialogue in collaborative problem-solving contexts, with an emphasis on the emerging dynamics of human-AI and multi-agent collaboration. translated_title: 连接言谈与思考：理解协作问题解决情境中的对话动态
translated_abstract: 我们提出一个用于分析协作问题解决情境中对话的概念框架，重点关注人机协作与多智能体协作的新兴动态。随着智能系统成为具备自主推理和战略合作能力的主动主体，理解协作问题解决过程中的对话交互对于优化和评估此类合作关系日益重要。我们的框架通过一个整合认知与非认知问题解决及元认知调节机制的分层双层编码方案，解决了当前分析方法的关键局限。我们在涵盖多个领域的九个数据集上展示了其有效性和泛化能力，并深入揭示了人类与智能体如何协调知识、技能和努力来解决复杂问题，尤其表明元认知调节可以成为高效协作的关键驱动力。
tldr: 本文提出一个整合认知、非认知与元认知调节的分层双层编码框架，用于分析人机协作问题解决中的对话动态，并在九个跨领域数据集上验证其有效性。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes a hierarchical two-layer coding framework integrating cognitive, non-cognitive, and metacognitive regulation to analyze dialogue dynamics in human-AI collaborative problem solving, validated across nine cross-domain datasets.
